body_txt: Alice tries to do something. She tries really hard, in a bunch of different ways, and none of them work. Finally, she succeeds — either in the one last half-hearted attempt she makes before giving up or precisely by giving up. This is a staple of Secret Test of Character plotlines. When done poorly, it can come across as the Broken Aesop version of Know When to Fold 'Em . May overlap with Politeness Judo , if the character only has to ask nicely to succeed. Contrast Gave Up Too Soon . Compare Sheathe Your Sword , Last Place You Look , Autopilot Artistry , and Wins by Doing Absolutely Nothing . The Centipede's Dilemma is often due to this.
